rationale: The Iran deal edging closer offers indirect energy price relief for Romania; the Russia-Ukraine war near Romania's border continues without new escalation.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: A potential US-Iran deal could lower energy import costs for Romania, which remains exposed to global gas and oil price swings affecting household bills.

top_events.title: Russia-Ukraine war / Black Sea security ongoing
top_events.summary: War near Romania's border and Black Sea continues without new escalation this cycle — persistent background risk but no fresh qualitative change.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: US-China tensions rising slightly, with marginal indirect effect on global trade and EU economic stability — low direct impact on Romanian daily life.
